negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: UCLA DGSOM visiting students hub restricts to LCME-accredited or COCA-accredited AACOM-member institutions, fourth-year only. International medical students are not addressed on this page; the international lane is not documented on the UCLA VSLO page.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Visa language absent; no explicit IMG / international student exclusion statement, but the LCME/AOA-only eligibility effectively excludes non-US students.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — UCLA DGSOM hub; RRUCLAMC explicitly named as placement site; (b) US LCME or COCA AACOM 4th-year only; (c) $300 per elective non-refundable; (d) max 9 weeks across up to 3 electives; (e) 75-day lead time; (f) AAMC Standard Immunization Form required. No international lane on UCLA main hub.
plainEnglishSummary: UCLA's David Geffen School of Medicine runs the visiting students program covering Ronald Reagan UCLA Medical Center placement. Only US 4th-year LCME or COCA AACOM-member students may apply via VSLO. Fee is $300 per elective non-refundable. Duration is 3-week blocks, max 3 electives or 9 weeks. 75-day lead time. No international or IMG lane is documented at UCLA main hub.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because California gained its second classified institution with verbatim audience + cost + duration + placement-site evidence; UCLA hub captures Ronald Reagan UCLA Medical Center as the explicitly-named placement site.
